title: Function Types, Parameters, Overloads, and Callable APIs · TypeScript Build Room
summary: Refactor status calculations and adapters into callable contracts that preserve inputs, failures, and return relationships. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a callable service transformation toolkit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model parameter, return, callback, this, and side-effect expectations with readable function types.
Reason about callback parameter compatibility under strictFunctionTypes without accepting unsound narrower handlers.
Model optional, default, rest, tuple-rest, and destructured parameters without ambiguous call shapes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
